reviews.review_msg: Very clear that Nilima is a master at numerical analysis. All lectures were written on a black board which is insane considering the material. Always answered emails on the day of sending, and was generally kind to students and super passionate about the course. Difficult homework, regularly took over 20 hours to complete and 50 percent was avg.
Professor Nilima is very kind and keen on answering questions. Lectures are based on textbooks and exams are fair. Definitely recommend !

This prof made me want to come to lecture, she genuinely wants us to understand and do well. Other comments are right that lectures can be disorganized at times, but she's always willing to double back if anyone is confused. Proof assignments sometimes had confusing grading, attend class for her written examples!
Best math prof @ SFU. Incredible lecturer, well-crafted assignments, very fair exams. She set aside lots of time for office hours each week. Clearly cared about our learning.

So much textbook reading. Lectures essentially cover 300 pages of text. Lectures disorganized visually with so much fast writing. "Study everything" for tests. Mean in office hours; made me feel stupid for asking questions and discouraged me from attending. 5 hours of OH a week was nice. LONG assignments, but MATLAB is cool. NOT LIKE MACM316 AT ALL

Very helpful outside of class at office hours, but I found the lectures and notes themselves to be very poorly organized. We would often be given the "key idea" to understanding a particular topic, but the surrounding details felt glossed over, making the idea effectively useless to know. I found the midterms to be very fair. 5/5 person 1/5 lecture

This is a great class and Nilima Nigam is a great professor. She explained a lot of hard stuff in a way such that people can understand and I benefitted a lot from it. Wish she also showed class averages for exams so we wouldn't be in the dark about how we're doing.

Professor Nigam is by far one of my favourite professors I've had so far in my degree. She is definitely passionate about math and that carries over into her teaching. Most of the lectures are spent deriving formulas and going over theory/concepts but there are more examples in her posted notes. Midterm was very fair but marked somewhat harshly

I did well in this class but more so out of my love for deeply understanding math and self-cleaning skills NOT the professor. This professor assaults the mental health and confidence of perfectly good and very intelligent students. It is unnecessary and she plays cruel psychological games with students. It is shameful.
